description: This plot presents the Vickers hardness evolution of Fe-22.5Mn-1.2Al-0.3C low-density steel as a function of annealing time under different cold rolling and annealing conditions. Three curves are included: black squares represent samples with 30% cold rolling reduction annealed at 630 °C, black circles represent samples with 40% cold rolling reduction annealed at 550 °C, black triangles represent samples with 50% cold rolling reduction annealed at 550 °C. The x-axis is annealing time in second with a logarithmic scale ranging from 0.1 s to 100000 s, and the y-axis is Vickers hardness (HV) ranging from 150 HV to 450 HV. Initial hardness increases with higher cold rolling reduction, starting at ~335 HV for 30% reduction, ~395 HV for 40% reduction, and ~425 HV for 50% reduction. For all samples, hardness decreases with increasing annealing time, with an obvious accelerated drop at longer annealing durations, which is attributed to recovery and recrystallization eliminating work hardening. This figure supports the conclusion that higher cold rolling reduction introduces stronger work hardening to improve hardness, while annealing induced recrystallization reduces hardness, providing reference for mechanical property regulation of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels via cold rolling and annealing process design.
